what_it_does: A token-driven CSS stylesheet that defines a family of visual specimen/preview layouts (the .sp-* namespace) used to render thumbnail-like previews of generated design/code artifacts, each filling its container.
observations: All specimen classes share the .sp prefix and use BEM-style naming (e.g., .sp-theme__bar).
Every specimen is designed to fill its container (width: 100%; height: 100%) and centers content with flexbox.
Colors, backgrounds, and borders are driven by CSS custom properties/tokens such as --accent-gold, --bg-surface, --ink, and --ms-hairline.
The file contains no @keyframes, transitions, or media queries; it is purely static layout/styling.
Several specimens use abstract skeleton shapes (i, span, div) as lines, bars, chips, and cards to represent real UI elements.
Modifier classes exist for notifications (.sp-note--success, --warning, --error) and widget variants (.sp-widget--bars).
Some specimens rely on absolute positioning (.sp-deck, .sp-image) and assume a specific container aspect ratio.
SVG styling is included for widget, motion, and diagram specimens, but no SVG markup is present in this file.
standouts: A complete CSS vocabulary of abstract visual specimens for artifact kinds (theme, palette, type, slide, deck, widget, screen, component, control, notification, motion, image, diagram, coachmark, form).
Heavy reliance on design tokens, with a shared gold accent color family across most specimens.
Skeleton-style previews that use simple geometric shapes to stand in for complex UI components.
conventions: Follows BEM-ish block-element-modifier naming with double underscores and double dashes.
Uses CSS custom properties for theming and avoids hardcoded colors in most rules.
Uses pixel-based sizing for fine-grained preview proportions.
Modifier classes are applied at the block level (e.g., .sp-note--success) or as state classes (e.g., .sp-control__seg.on).
Decorative line elements are styled with i tags, which is non-semantic but contained to previews.
concerns: Hardcoded pixel dimensions may not scale gracefully across different container sizes.
Absolute positioning in .sp-deck and .sp-image depends on the container having predictable dimensions.
No focus, hover, or active states are defined, limiting accessibility/interactivity.
The .sp-motion class implies animation but no animation rules are present in this file.
.sp-control__seg.on applies border-radius only to the active segment, which may produce inconsistent rounded corners depending on segment position.
Color contrast and accessibility depend entirely on external token values not visible here.
notes: This file is a compact, token-driven CSS catalog of miniature skeleton previews for artifact kinds, meant to be rendered inside small containers.
questions: Where are the CSS custom properties (--accent-gold, --bg-surface, etc.) defined?
Is there a runtime mapping from artifact kind names to these .sp-* classes?
Are these specimens static thumbnails or intended to be interactive?
How is the .sp-motion specimen animated, given no keyframes are present?
Is there a companion JSX/TSX file that consumes these styles?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches: the file is a token-driven CSS catalog of miniature visual previews/skeletons for artifact kinds.
intention_signals: The file appears to be building a reusable visual language for quickly previewing categories of generated artifacts without needing real content, enabling a gallery or ledger of artifact kinds.
novelty: A dedicated stylesheet of pure CSS 'specimens' that use geometric shapes and skeleton lines to represent widgets, screens, slides, diagrams, motion curves, and other design artifacts.
contribution: A self-contained, token-driven set of visual specimen styles that can render artifact previews inside arbitrary containers; useful as a reference catalog of preview layouts for a fused design system.
summary_for_embedding: A CSS file defining the .sp-* KindSpecimen component styles, providing token-driven, container-filling skeleton previews for generated design and code artifacts.
intention_for_embedding: To supply a reusable CSS vocabulary for rendering static, thumbnail-like visual previews of artifact kinds in a design-system ledger or gallery.
